Title: Resolving floral development dynamics using genome and single-cell temporal transcriptome of Dendrobium devonianum

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: highly expressed in trichome cell clusters EC_2 and EC_3; increases along pseudotime; highly expressed at S15; also expressed in vascular-related clusters
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GL1-1 marked trichome cell sub-clusters during epidermal re-clustering. 2. It showed a pseudotime pattern associated with differentiation toward fringe/trichome cells. 3. The study suggests GL1-1 may play a significant role in labellum fringe development.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: associated with promoting fringe/trichome differentiation on the labellum margin
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: scRNA-seq clustering; pseudotime analysis; stage-specific expression analysis
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: candidate gene for modifying ornamental fringe traits
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: trait is structural rather than nutrient metabolite related, but gene is a core experimental subject in results

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: highly expressed in trichome cell clusters EC_2 and EC_3; highly expressed in late developmental stages
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GL3 was highly expressed in trichome-like epidermal sub-clusters. 2. It tracked with trichome/fringe differentiation during pseudotime. 3. The study suggests GL3 may contribute significantly to fringe development.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: scRNA-seq clustering; pseudotime analysis; stage-specific expression analysis
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: candidate target for modifying fringe density or morphology
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: structural developmental regulator, not metabolite gene

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: shared pseudotime curve with GL1-1 and MADS6; highly expressed in later stage S15; enriched in gene clusters C1/C4
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. KAS12 showed pseudotime dynamics associated with fringe development. 2. It was highly expressed in later flower stages. 3. The study proposed that KAS12 may contribute to structural integrity and functionality of fringes.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: scRNA-seq pseudotime analysis; stage-specific expression analysis
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: linked to lipid biosynthetic process GO enrichment in fringe lineage

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: labellum fringe-associated epidermal and vascular-related cells
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: similar pseudotime curve to GL1-1 and KAS12; highly expressed in later stage S15; also expressed in vascular-related clusters
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MADS6 followed a pseudotime pattern associated with fringe development. 2. It was highly expressed in later developmental stages. 3. The study suggests MADS6 may contribute to the structural integrity and functionality of fringes.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: scRNA-seq pseudotime analysis; cell-type expression mapping
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: candidate regulator for specialized floral morphology breeding
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: although a TF, the paper treated it within fringe developmental candidates rather than nutrient metabolism regulation

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: shared pseudotime curve with GL1-1, MADS6 and KAS12; enriched in gene clusters C1/C4
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ABCG36 exhibited pseudotime dynamics associated with epidermal differentiation toward fringes. 2. It clustered with other candidate fringe-development genes. 3. The study suggests it may have a significant role in fringes development.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: candidate for floral epidermis trait engineering

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: hydroxylates carotenoid ring structures during xanthophyll formation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: significantly up-regulated in yellow blotches of the labellum
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DedeCYP97C1 was significantly up-regulated in the yellow blotches of the labellum. 2. Its expression was consistent with elevated zeaxanthin and violaxanthin accumulation in yellow regions. 3. It was identified as a key enzyme gene associated with floral yellow coloration.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: associated with promotion of yellow labellum pigmentation via carotenoid accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: spatial bulk RNA-seq expression profiling; MALDI-MSI metabolite localization; pathway annotation
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: flower developmental stage and labellum colour region
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: candidate target for engineering stronger yellow blotches through enhanced carotenoid flux
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: identified from genome-wide pigment pathway mining; causal role inferred from spatial association rather than direct transgenics

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: hydroxylates β-carotene to produce xanthophyll carotenoids including zeaxanthin
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: significantly up-regulated in yellow blotches of the labellum
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DedeBCH2 was significantly up-regulated in yellow labellum blotches. 2. Its expression matched zeaxanthin/violaxanthin enrichment in yellow tissue. 3. It was highlighted as a key carotenoid biosynthesis gene underlying yellow colour formation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: promotes yellow coloration through increased xanthophyll accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: spatial bulk RNA-seq expression profiling; MALDI-MSI metabolite localization; pathway annotation
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: flower developmental stage and labellum colour region
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: useful target for enhancing yellow ornamental pigmentation
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: paper emphasized BCH among known yellow-region-upregulated genes in other species as well

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: isomerizes cis-carotene intermediates to all-trans-lycopene in carotenoid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: significantly up-regulated in yellow blotches; more highly expressed at later developmental stages; enriched in vascular bundle cells at S15
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DedeCRTISO1 was significantly up-regulated in yellow labellum blotches. 2. It showed stronger expression at later stages of flower development. 3. scRNA-seq localized DedeCRTISO1 enrichment to vascular bundle cells at S15, supporting spatially restricted carotenoid synthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: associated with increased yellow-region carotenoid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: spatial and temporal bulk RNA-seq; scRNA-seq cell-type localization; MALDI-MSI metabolite localization
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: flower developmental stage and labellum colour region
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: candidate for increasing carotenoid flux into visible yellow pigmentation
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: one of the few pigment genes resolved both spatially and at cell-type level

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: hydroxylates carotenoid ring structures during xanthophyll biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: significantly up-regulated in yellow blotches of the labellum
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DedeCYP97B3 was significantly up-regulated in yellow blotches. 2. Its expression paralleled carotenoid enrichment in yellow tissue. 3. It was identified as a candidate structural gene for yellow spot formation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: spatial bulk RNA-seq expression profiling; MALDI-MSI metabolite localization; pathway annotation
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: flower developmental stage and labellum colour region
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: candidate for carotenoid engineering of yellow ornamental traits
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: functional assignment based on homology and pigment-correlated expression

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: epsilon-cyclization branch leading to xanthophyll composition
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: cyclizes lycopene to introduce an epsilon ring and shape carotenoid branch composition
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: significantly up-regulated in yellow blotches; enriched in vascular bundle cells at S15
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DedeLCYE1 was significantly up-regulated in yellow blotches of the labellum. 2. scRNA-seq showed enrichment in vascular bundle cells at S15. 3. It was implicated in spatially restricted carotenoid biosynthesis associated with yellow colour development.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: associated with yellow blotch carotenoid accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: spatial bulk RNA-seq; scRNA-seq cell-type localization; MALDI-MSI metabolite localization
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: flower developmental stage and labellum colour region
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: candidate for fine-tuning carotenoid branch flux in ornamental breeding
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: paper links gene expression to yellow spot formation but no direct biochemical assay in this species

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: desaturates phytoene to downstream carotenoid intermediates en route to colored carotenoids
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DedePDS1 was significantly up-regulated in the yellow blotches of the labellum. 2. Co-expression analysis suggested it may be regulated by multiple TF families. 3. It was implicated as a structural carotenoid gene contributing to yellow pigmentation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: associated with enhanced carotenoid biosynthesis in yellow tissue
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: spatial bulk RNA-seq; co-expression network analysis; MALDI-MSI metabolite localization
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: flower developmental stage and labellum colour region
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: MYBs; AP2/ERFs; bHLHs; NACs; bZIPs; WRKYs (co-expression candidates)
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: candidate upstream flux-control gene for carotenoid enhancement
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: identified as potentially TF-regulated in correlation network
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: bulk RNA-seq; co-expression network; MALDI-MSI; scRNA-seq PRJCA033181

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: condenses two geranylgeranyl diphosphate molecules to form phytoene, the first committed carotenoid precursor
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: significantly up-regulated in yellow blotches of the labellum
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DedePSY2 was significantly up-regulated in yellow blotches of the labellum. 2. Its expression pattern was consistent with increased zeaxanthin and violaxanthin in yellow regions. 3. It was highlighted as a key carotenoid structural gene associated with yellow colour formation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: promotes carotenoid accumulation associated with yellow blotches
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: spatial bulk RNA-seq expression profiling; MALDI-MSI metabolite localization; pathway annotation
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: flower developmental stage and labellum colour region
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: high-value engineering target for strengthening yellow carotenoid pigmentation
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: paper cites PSY as commonly yellow-region induced in prior studies

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: methylates hydroxylated flavonoid or anthocyanin intermediates, contributing to pigment diversification
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for modification of target product
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: significantly up-regulated in purple regions of the labellum
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DedeOMT2 was significantly up-regulated in the purple regions of the labellum. 2. It was associated with anthocyanin pathway activity promoting purple colour formation. 3. Spatial expression matched purple-region anthocyanin accumulation detected by MALDI-MSI.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: promotes purple coloration via anthocyanin/flavonoid modification
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: spatial bulk RNA-seq expression profiling; MALDI-MSI metabolite localization; pathway annotation
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: flower developmental stage and labellum colour region
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: candidate target for modifying hue intensity in ornamental breeding
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: functional assignment inferred from pathway annotation and region-specific expression

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: glycosylates anthocyanidin or flavonoid aglycones to stabilize and accumulate pigments
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for storage/modification of target product
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: significantly up-regulated in purple regions of the labellum
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DedeUGT72L7 was significantly up-regulated in purple labellum regions. 2. It was implicated in anthocyanin/flavonoid modification linked to purple pigmentation. 3. Its spatial expression was consistent with anthocyanin-rich purple tissue.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: supports accumulation of purple anthocyanin pigments
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: spatial bulk RNA-seq expression profiling; MALDI-MSI metabolite localization; pathway annotation
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: flower developmental stage and labellum colour region
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: candidate for improving pigment stability and visible purple intensity
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: UGT72L family also expanded in genome according to duplication analysis

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: oxidizes leucoanthocyanidins to anthocyanidins in late anthocyanin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: significantly up-regulated in purple regions of the labellum
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DedeLDOX3 was significantly up-regulated in purple regions of the labellum. 2. It was associated with anthocyanin production underlying purple colour formation. 3. Spatial expression aligned with anthocyanin-enriched purple tissue.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: spatial bulk RNA-seq expression profiling; MALDI-MSI metabolite localization; pathway annotation
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: flower developmental stage and labellum colour region
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: strong candidate for engineering purple floral pigmentation
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: one of the explicitly highlighted purple-region ABP genes

Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: predominantly expressed in epidermal cells at stage S15; co-expression network candidate
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DedeBCH1 was predominantly expressed in epidermal cells at S15. 2. Co-expression analysis identified it among structural genes potentially regulated by TFs. 3. It likely contributes to spatially restricted carotenoid accumulation in epidermal tissue.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: supports carotenoid biosynthesis in pigment-producing epidermal cells
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: scRNA-seq cell-type localization; co-expression network analysis; pathway annotation
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: MYBs; AP2/ERFs; bHLHs; NACs; bZIPs; WRKYs (co-expression candidates)
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: candidate for enhancing epidermis-specific yellow pigmentation
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: BCH1 was emphasized in single-cell rather than region-level expression
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: scRNA-seq PRJCA033181; co-expression network; bulk RNA-seq

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: hydroxylates the B ring of flavonoid intermediates to support cyanidin-type anthocyanin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: predominantly expressed in epidermal cells at S15
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DedeF3'H1 was predominantly expressed in epidermal cells at S15. 2. It was identified as a key anthocyanin/flavonoid biosynthetic gene in cell-type analysis. 3. Its localization supports epidermis-restricted pigment synthesis linked to purple coloration.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: supports anthocyanin biosynthesis in purple-pigmented epidermal cells
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: scRNA-seq cell-type localization; bulk RNA-seq pathway analysis
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: candidate for tuning purple hue through hydroxylation pattern control
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: paper emphasized cell-type specificity rather than direct purple-region differential expression for this gene

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: labellum purple and yellow patterned regions
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: one or more structural pigment genes; positive correlations reported with structural genes
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: positive correlation with structural pigment biosynthetic genes
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: highly expressed during late developmental stages; highly expressed in TF cluster 5 of epidermal pseudotime analysis as MYB305
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DedeMYB305-1 showed strong positive correlations with one or more pigment structural genes. 2. It was highly expressed during late flower developmental stages. 3. The study proposed it as a key regulator coordinating pigment-related gene activity across development.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: likely promotes anthocyanin/carotenoid biosynthesis associated with floral coloration
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: co-expression network analysis; temporal expression profiling
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: promising regulator for coordinated enhancement of floral pigment traits
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: proposed key regulator from co-expression network
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: MYB305 also appeared in fringe-development pseudotime TF cluster, suggesting broader epidermal developmental roles
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: bulk RNA-seq; scRNA-seq PRJCA033181; co-expression network

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: labellum purple and yellow patterned regions
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: one or more structural pigment genes; positive correlations reported with structural genes
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: positive correlation with structural pigment biosynthetic genes
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: highly expressed during late developmental stages
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DedebHLH104 showed strong positive correlations with one or more structural pigment genes. 2. It was highly expressed during late flower developmental stages. 3. The study highlighted it as a likely key regulator of floral pigment biosynthesis.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: likely promotes pigment biosynthesis contributing to floral colour patterning
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: co-expression network analysis; temporal expression profiling
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: possible MYB-bHLH regulatory module, not directly validated
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: candidate master regulator for engineering coordinated floral pigmentation
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: co-expression evidence suggests major positive regulator
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: explicitly named in conclusion as example of key regulator
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: bulk RNA-seq; co-expression network; scRNA-seq PRJCA033181

Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: one or more structural pigment genes
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: highly expressed during late developmental stages
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DedeMYB308 showed strong positive correlations with structural pigment genes. 2. It was highly expressed during late developmental stages. 3. It was identified as a candidate positive regulator of anthocyanin/carotenoid biosynthesis.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: co-expression network analysis; temporal expression profiling
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: candidate regulator for pigment intensity improvement
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: identified from network, not direct target-binding assays

Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: one or more structural pigment genes
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: highly expressed during late developmental stages
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DedebHLH62-2 showed strong positive correlations with one or more structural pigment genes. 2. It was highly expressed during late stages. 3. It was identified as a candidate positive regulator of floral pigmentation.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: likely promotes pigment biosynthesis associated with colour patterning
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: co-expression network analysis; temporal expression profiling
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: possible MYB-bHLH module, not directly tested
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: candidate positive regulator for ornamental pigment engineering

Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DedeMYB17 exhibited negative correlations with structural pigment genes. 2. The study proposed that it may act as a repressor. 3. It may antagonize anthocyanin/carotenoid structural gene expression during flower pigmentation.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: possible knockout target to relieve repression and intensify floral pigmentation
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: repressor role inferred solely from negative correlation
